{{Infobox concept
| name = Lichborn
| introduced = The Captain
}}

''See also: [[Lichborn Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Lichborn are a species characterized by their gray skin and eyes that emit a green light. Originally custom-designed by humans to be flawless, they possess an appearance that is considered exceptionally beautiful by human standards. This physical perfection and natural charisma have allowed members of the species to find success in political roles and within the [[Subline]]. They are noted for an unnatural resilience, which historically allowed them to survive significant damage that would be fatal to humans. While they can accumulate scars like warriors, some individuals possess a flame of resurrection that prevents such markings. The Lichborn homeworld is [[Dornoth]] Four, a planet inhabited by approximately one hundred million people, though billions more reside on space stations throughout the system. The species is also known to inhabit other locations, such as [[Dornoth]] II. Their culture and technology reflect a skeletal aesthetic; their spacecraft are designed to look like bones and utilize green light or fire for illumination. While they once used magic to power their vessels, modern ships rely on different methods. The Lichborn have a history of long-standing warfare with [[Karosha]] and have faced recent instability due to the appearance of the [[Iron Legion]] in their solar system. In addition to their natural traits, some Lichborn are integrated into human-led organizations. For example, certain individuals serve as [[Advocates]], utilizing embedded [[Aethertech]] to enhance their abilities. There are also hybrid human-Lichborn individuals within the population.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The Lichborn are a people hailing from their own homeworld, characterized by gray skin and eyes that shine with green light. These individuals possess specific marks within their eyes and are considered almost universally beautiful by human standards. While they find a sense of home in hostile hellscapes, the Lichborn are currently engaged in a war against the Karoshans. Beyond the people themselves, the name also refers to a system that exports thought-conduction alloys.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lichborn]] people have gray skin and eyes that shine with green light.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c5">''The Captain'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lichborn]] find hostile hellscapes to be home.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The opponent in the fighter was an older [[Lichborn]] man.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11">''The Captain'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Lichborn]] have specific marks on their eyes.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18">''The Captain'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lichborn]] are almost universally beautiful by human standards.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19">''The Captain'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lichborn]] is a type of person or cadet.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c22">''The Captain'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lichborn]] is a system that exports thought-conduction alloys.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Lichborn]] are at war with the Karoshans.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38">''The Captain'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lichborn]] is a homeworld.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44">''The Captain'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Sola]] possesses the eyes of a healthy [[Lichborn]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44" />}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The Lichborn are a group of people who have been engaged in a generational war against [[Karosha]]. Within this race, [[Moonmaster]] possesses Lichborn heritage as a half-Lichborn. During his journey, [[Starhammer]] contemplates the possibility that his savior belongs to this group. The Lichborn are represented by a woman clad in [[Aethertech armor]], though a Lichborn man was also present during the encounter.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Lichborn]] have been at war with [[Karosha]] for generations.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c5">''The Engineer'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Moonmaster]] is a half-[[Lichborn]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6">''The Engineer'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Lichborn]] is a heritage or race that [[Moonmaster]] possesses.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] considers the possibility that his savior might be a [[Lichborn]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Lichborn]] are a group of people.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c13">''The Engineer'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Lichborn]] is a woman wearing [[Aethertech armor]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c14">''The Engineer'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|A [[Lichborn]] man was present during the encounter.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c43">''The Engineer'', chapter 43</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The Lichborn are one of the five species of intelligent beings found throughout the galaxy. Characterized by their black hair and gray skin, members of this race possess the unique ability to survive specific levels of damage. Their presence is noted in various cultural depictions, including statues of [[arena]] fighters. On one occasion, a [[Lichborn woman]] arrived at a landing site while a holographic screen displayed a royal drama involving her kind.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Lichborn]] is a species or type of person with gray skin and black hair.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c2">''The Knight'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Lichborn]] are one of the five species of intelligent beings in the galaxy.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c8">''The Knight'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A [[Lichborn woman]] arrives at the landing site.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9">''The Knight'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Lichborn]] is a type of being that can survive certain levels of damage.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c11">''The Knight'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Lichborn]] is a type of being or race.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c12">''The Knight'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Lichborn]] are among the types of people depicted in [[arena]] fighter statues.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c25">''The Knight'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A [[Lichborn]] royal drama was being displayed on a holographic screen.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28">''The Knight'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Lichborn are a species characterized by their near-flawless appearance and gray skin. Originating from their homeworld on [[Dornoth IV]], they have expanded their reach by initiating mining operations within the [[Grand Hive]]. As a group capable of manufacturing both ships and [[life]]-pods, their fleet can travel anywhere via subspace warp. This group, which includes individuals such as [[Nera Jesh]] and a gray-streaked woman seen in the hangar, possesses a history of utilizing power armor. While they are capable of encircling others in anger, certain members like [[Sola]] possess distinct green eyes.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Lichborn]] have their homeworld on [[Dornoth IV]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c10">''The Pilot'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Lichborn]] have begun mining operations in the [[Grand Hive]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Lichborn]] are a fleet that emerged from a [[Subspace warp]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lichborn]] is a group that manufactures [[life]]-pods and ships.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16">''The Pilot'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Lichborn fleet]] is capable of taking [[Ark]] anywhere.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A [[Lichborn woman]] with gray-streaked hair is present in the hangar.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]] has a green [[Lichborn]] eye.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c19">''The Pilot'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nera Jesh]] is a [[Lichborn]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c31">''The Pilot'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Lichborn]] have a history of power armor.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lichborn]] are a species with gray skin and near-flawless looks.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c37">''The Pilot'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lichborn]] can be angry and encircle others.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c37" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon concepts]]
[[Category:Lifeforms]]
